task_completion.description: Navigate to the Users administration page in Redmine.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the Redmine 'Users' administration page, displaying a table of users with columns such as Login, Name, Email, and Last connection.
The user 'bwayne' is shown with a 'Locked' status in the Users list, or a screenshot shows the agent editing 'bwayne' and setting their status to 'Locked'.
The user 'ckent' is shown with a 'Locked' status in the Users list, or a screenshot shows the agent editing 'ckent' and setting their status to 'Locked'.

task_completion.description: Ensure the safe users 'dprince' and 'ballen' remain active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Users list, the accounts for 'dprince' and 'ballen' display an 'Active' status (or do not show a 'Locked' badge/icon).
In the Users list, the 'admin' account displays an 'Active' status (or does not show a 'Locked' badge/icon).

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser interface to lock users.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Redmine web UI (clicking links, dropdowns, or buttons) rather than using a terminal to run database commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Redmine application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shows the authentic Redmine layout, styling, and navigation menus, rather than a text editor or a locally mocked HTML file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the locked status.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element) being open to manually alter the text from 'Active' to 'Locked' in the DOM.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The cutoff date is 2026-01-01. Target users that MUST be locked are 'bwayne' (last login 2025-10-15) and 'ckent' (last login 2025-12-20). Safe users that MUST remain active are 'dprince' (last login 2026-02-14), 'ballen' (last login 2026-03-01), and 'admin'. In Redmine, active status corresponds to internal code 1, and locked status corresponds to internal code 3. Visually, locked users will display a 'Locked' status or an 'Unlock' button in the Users list, while active users will display an 'Active' status.
